title: Factory Heat-Exchanger Web
seed_problem: heat-integration / pinch analysis: match hot and cold process streams with exchangers to minimize external hot+cold utility under a minimum-approach constraint
mutation: composite: the network is emitted as an ORDERED stream of exchanger matches replayed against per-stream running temperatures (temperature-feasibility-ordering); each stream's duty may be SPLIT across many matches (stream-split-matching); the objective is total external utility (utility-minimization). Instances are planted with a sharp pinch so the obvious hottest-to-coldest greedy transfers heat across it and strands whole clusters.
innovation_hook: A single discovered scalar -- the pinch temperature -- partitions the instance into two independent subproblems with a provable utility floor; never moving heat across it converts messy matching into two tractable halves.
strategies: trivial: no exchangers; all heat bought as external utility (reproduces baseline B -> 0.1)
greedy (trap): pair the hottest hot with the coldest cold and dump max feasible heat; drives high-cluster heat across the pinch, stranding high-cluster colds and low-cluster hots -> recovers ~half

strong (insight): serve each cold with the COLDEST adequate hot (minimum driving force); this confines every match within its temperature cluster and never crosses the pinch -- one-to-one, no splitting
beyond strong (head-room): pinch decomposition + optimal split-matching / residual re-distribution recovers the hot heat the one-to-one match leaves stranded
